pattern: FHE feature extraction — encrypt at source
gdpr_requirement: Data protection by design — minimise processing of personal data at point of collection
implementation_note: Raw biomedical features encrypted with CKKS before any computation; plaintext values never leave origin system

gdpr_requirement: Data protection by design — limit processing to what is necessary for specified purposes
implementation_note: Statistical operations (sum/mean/variance/dot-product) execute entirely in ciphertext; no intermediate decryption

gdpr_requirement: Data minimisation — adequate and limited to what is necessary
implementation_note: Only derived feature vector decrypted; raw input values never exposed to downstream processors

gdpr_requirement: Accountability — controller able to demonstrate compliance
implementation_note: DataMinimizationPipeline.get_audit_log() records per-request provenance including encryption parameters

gdpr_requirement: Purpose limitation — collected for specified purposes and not further processed incompatibly
implementation_note: Context parameters (poly_modulus_degree) tied to declared computation purpose; re-use requires new context

gdpr_requirement: Data protection by design — built-in technical safeguards
implementation_note: Per-example gradient clipping (max_grad_norm) bounds individual contribution before parameter update

gdpr_requirement: Integrity and confidentiality — appropriate technical measures
implementation_note: BudgetAccountant enforces hard stop at declared (epsilon delta) budget; training halts on exhaustion

gdpr_requirement: Accountability — demonstrate compliance with data-protection principles
implementation_note: BudgetAccountant.get_summary() persists per-epoch epsilon/delta values to structured audit log

gdpr_requirement: Pseudonymisation and safeguards for research processing
implementation_note: Gaussian mechanism adds calibrated noise ensuring outputs cannot be attributed to any individual training record

gdpr_requirement: Appropriate safeguards for processing for archiving/research purposes — technical measures required
implementation_note: DP mechanism satisfies Art. 89(1) technical-safeguard requirement for statistical research processing

gdpr_requirement: Integrity and confidentiality — protection against unauthorised processing
implementation_note: Prompt-injection test suite detects attempts to extract training data through adversarial inputs

gdpr_requirement: Data protection by design — prevent re-identification
implementation_note: MembershipInferenceAttack baseline verifies model confidence scores cannot confirm/deny individual record presence

gdpr_requirement: Integrity and confidentiality — prevent accidental disclosure
implementation_note: Log-capture test cases verify inference pipeline does not echo sensitive context through log channels

gdpr_requirement: Data protection impact assessment — systematic description of processing operations
implementation_note: AssessmentRunner outputs structured JSON report documenting risk level and mitigation evidence per test

pattern: LLM leakage — data subject rights impact
gdpr_requirement: Automated decision-making — human oversight and right to explanation
implementation_note: Assessment checklist includes review of whether LLM outputs constitute automated decisions with legal effects

gdpr_requirement: Purpose limitation + research safeguards — documented legal basis for processing
implementation_note: IRB amendment template captures GDPR legal basis (Art. 89 research exemption) alongside institutional approval

gdpr_requirement: Data protection by design — use synthetic data for development and testing
implementation_note: generate_synthetic_clinical.py produces statistically plausible records with no link to real data subjects

gdpr_requirement: Data minimisation — ensure only necessary data is processed
implementation_note: Governance checklist operationalises data-minimisation principle for research team onboarding

gdpr_requirement: Data protection by design and by default + security of processing
implementation_note: Four-layer governed-data design (collection / encryption / compute / output review) embeds GDPR controls at architecture level

gdpr_requirement: Security of processing — ensure ongoing confidentiality and integrity of processing systems
implementation_note: Reference architecture specifies role-based access control at each layer boundary before data is permitted to flow

gdpr_requirement: Data minimisation + privacy by design — restrict outputs to aggregate results
implementation_note: PI review gate before export ensures only statistically aggregated results leave secure compute environment

gdpr_requirement: Transfers of personal data to third countries — appropriate safeguards required
implementation_note: Architecture explicitly flags cross-jurisdiction transfer points; FHE ensures ciphertext-only movement where raw transfer prohibited
